---
title: "Week 10 — Probability as risk and diagnosis"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

Could this result be due to chance alone?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Chance models, observed statistics, and the logic of a randomization
test.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Read a StatKey randomization-test screenshot and interpret the null
distribution and p-value.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab on a new StatKey output;
short p-value-in-context application.

## Primary source basis

OpenIntro IMS Ch 11 (foundations-randomization), re-cast with StatKey
artifacts.

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Curated StatKey screenshot library; no student-side R or programming.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Describe the logic of a randomization test.
- [ ] Identify the observed statistic.
- [ ] Interpret a p-value in context.
- [ ] Avoid ritualistic "reject / fail to reject" language as the only
      conclusion.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
